subject: Product Management
title: YouTube
description: A fast breakdown of YouTube for builders.

hook.text: YouTube launched in 2005 as a video dating site. Nobody uploaded dating videos. Everyone uploaded everything else.

buildup.text: The founders — Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim — pivoted to general video sharing when dating flopped.

discovery.text: YouTube's magic was the embed code. Anyone could put a video on any website, turning the entire web into distribution.

twist.text: Competitors like Google Video required downloads. YouTube played instantly in the browser. That one UX choice was decisive.

climax.text: Google bought YouTube for $1.65 billion after just 18 months. It now has 2 billion monthly users and hosts more video than TV.

punchline.text: Let anyone embed your product everywhere. Distribution solves itself.

quiz.question: What product decision gave YouTube its distribution advantage?
options: The embed code — letting anyone put videos on any website
